_meta.author: independent analyst pass (no Navigator code/output used)
_meta.grounding: all numbers verified directly from view data; design respects profiler flags
_meta.objective: Run a 5-facility hospital network at safe capacity and adequate staffing, and intervene where forecasts show risk.

data_truths_I_established_first.occupancy_reconciliation: FLAG: 74.3% matches neither 607/898 (67.6%) nor 607/1050 (57.8%). Do not present 74.3% as bed utilization without a caveat.
data_truths_I_established_first.occupancy_varies_by: FACILITY only (73.8%-74.9%, ~1pt spread). Does NOT vary by department (degenerate) - never break occupancy down by department.
data_truths_I_established_first.staffing_shortage_varies_by: DEPARTMENT (ICU -0.84, Emergency -0.76 worst) - this IS the actionable breakdown.
data_truths_I_established_first.referral_mix: FLAG: 5 statuses all ~20% (CV=0.01) - statistically uniform / likely synthetic. Do NOT headline a 'largest segment'.
data_truths_I_established_first.time_grain: Operational daily Apr 1 - May 9 2026 (~6 weeks); referrals monthly (12 months).

personas.rationale: Network-wide health in one screen. Headlines + risk, no operational detail.

kpis.name: Bed Utilization (occupied of staffed)
kpis.note: Computed from verified bed identity, NOT the unreconciled 74.3% figure.

personas.rationale: Which of the 5 facilities needs attention. Facility is the ONLY informative breakdown for occupancy.

kpis.name: Occupancy by Facility
kpis.note: Spread is only ~1pt - I would label this 'facilities are balanced' rather than implying a hotspot.

kpis.name: Staffed-bed shortfall vs licensed
kpis.note: Derived insight: 1050-898. A capacity lever no single view states.

personas.rationale: Owns staffing gaps, overtime, productivity, and the department-level shortage forecast - the real actionable layer.

kpis.name: Overtime Rate
kpis.note: High - worth flagging as the productivity risk.

kpis.name: Predicted Staffing Shortage by Department
kpis.note: THE headline actionable insight: ICU and Emergency drive the shortage. This breakdown is real (varies by dept).

personas.rationale: Owns the inbound pipeline. The volume trend matters; the status mix does NOT (uniform).

kpis.name: Monthly Referral Volume
kpis.note: Down 28% Jan->Dec - THIS is the story, a declining pipeline that will pressure future occupancy.

kpis.name: Referral Status Mix
kpis.note: Deliberately NOT charted as an insight. The 5 statuses are ~20% each (uniform/synthetic). Headlining 'Escalated is largest (16,111)' would be misleading.

what_I_did_that_navigator_did_not: Established a verified entity model + bed identity BEFORE designing anything.
Computed TRUE utilization (67.6%) instead of repeating the unreconciled 74.3%.
Refused to break occupancy down by department (degenerate) - used facility instead.
Surfaced the real actionable insight (ICU/Emergency shortage), which DOES vary by dept.
Refused to headline the referral status mix (uniform/synthetic) - omitted it with a reason.
Derived a net-new insight (152 licensed beds unstaffed) no single view states.
Attached confidence bounds as bands to their forecasts - never as standalone KPIs/persona.
